LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS PETERSEN-DEAN, INC., PETERSEN-DEAN COMMERCIAL, INC., TRI-VALLEY SUPPLY, INC. DBA TRI-VALLEY WHOLESALE'S AMENDED MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE TO ALAMEDA AND FOR ATTORNEYS FEES. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED IN PART. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA SUPERIOR COURT, BUT DEFENDANTS ARE NOT AWARDED ANY FEES. DEFENDANTS SATISFIED THEIR BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT VENUE IN SAN FRANCISCO IS IMPROPER. THE VENUE SELECTION CLAUSE IN THE BLANKET PURCHASE ORDER, EVEN IF APPLICABLE TO EITHER OR BOTH OF THE TRANSACTIONS AT ISSUE IN THIS CASE, IS UNENFORCEABLE AS AGAINST PUBLIC POLICY. PLAINTIFF'S SELECTION OF SAN FRANCISCO WAS NOT MADE IN BAD FAITH OR WHOLLY LACKING IN A PLAUSIBLE FACTUAL THEORY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, NOT REPORTED

(Renotice) Of Applicatio Nand Hearing For Right To Attach Order
Off Calendar Mar-22-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SUNTECH AMERICA, INC.'S Renoticed Application For Right To Attach Order is Off calendar. Motion to Transfer Venue was granted on March 15, 2012. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

Amended Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue (Improper Court) And For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Mar-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAY M. ROSS OF HOPKINS & CARLEY, TEL. NO. (408)286-9800 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETERSEN-DEAN, INC., PETERSEN-DEAN COMMERCIAL, INC., TRI-VALLEY SUPPLY, INC. DBA TRI-VALLEY WHOLESALE'S Amended Motion To Transfer Venue to Alameda And For Attorneys Fees IS Granted in part. The case is transferred to Alameda Superior Court, but defendants are not awarded any fees. Defendants satisfied their burden of showing that venue in San Francisco is improper. The venue selection clause in the blanket purchase order, even if applicable to either or both of the transactions at issue in this case, is unenforceable as against public policy. Plaintiff's selection of San Francisco was not made in bad faith or wholly lacking in a plausible factual theory. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Not reported. = (302/HEK)

(Renotice) Of Applicatio Nand Hearing For Right To Attach Order
Off Calendar Mar-02-2012 Continued To Mar-22-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF SUNTECH AMERICA, INC.'S Re-noticed Application and Hearing For Right To Attach Order is Continued to March 22, 2012 per hearing held on Feb. 29, 2012. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

Amended Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue (Improper Court) And For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Feb-29-2012 Continued To Mar-15-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Jay M. Ross, Esq., of Hopkins & Carley, Tel. 408.286.9800, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Eric J. Wiesner, Esq., of Morrison & Foerster LLP, Tel. 415.268.6119, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. The Court vacated its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS PETERSEN-DEAN, INC., PETERSEN-DEAN COMMERCIAL, INC. and TRI-VALLEY SUPPLY, INC. DBA TRI-VALLEY WHOLESALE'S Amended Notice Of Motion To Transfer Venue (Improper Court) And For Attorneys Fees is continued to March 15, 2012. Supplemental opposition is due 3/2/12. Supplemental reply is due 3/09/12. In addition, the court continued the Hearing on Plaintiff's Application for Right to Attach currently scheduled for March 2, 2012 to March 22, 2012. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT VACATED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS : PLAINTIFF SUNTECH AMERICA, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER is continued to March 21, 2011 to be heard at the same time as the Motion for Change of Venue at the 9:30 a.m. calendar. No opposition was filed. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported =(301/PJB)
